answer:
**Chapter 73 of the *Viṣṇu Smṛti* details the ritual procedure for performing a *śrāddha* (ancestral offering), a funerary rite to honor deceased ancestors.**[^1]

It covers preparatory steps such as inviting and seating Brāhmaṇas on the preceding day or during specified times; assigning representatives (two facing east for the gods, three facing north for the ancestors, or one each); offering oblations into the fire with specific mantra groups depending on the *śrāddha* type (e.g., uncooked, animal, new moon, or festivals like Āgrahāyaṇī and Aṣṭakā); invoking ancestors; dismissing demons; providing *pādya*, *arghya*, worship items; making three oblations; serving food to representatives (Viśvedevas east, paternal ancestors north); forming *piṇḍas* (rice balls) for father, grandfather, and great-grandfather on southward *kuśa* grass; giving garments, food, and rubbing hands; offering water, flowers, etc.; post-meal rites like rinsing and sprinkling; honoring Brāhmaṇas with gifts; and dismissing them with blessings for prosperity, offspring, and generosity.[^2][^3]

[^1]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, Chapter 73, [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/73/translation/).
[^2]: From the chapter opening: "Now, one who wishes to perform a *śrāddha* shall invite Brāhmaṇas on the preceding day." [p-93388a](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/73/translation/#p-93388a).
[^3]: Key passage (TextRank #1): "Two for the rite for the gods, facing east, and three for the rite for the ancestors, facing north." [p-963793](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/73/translation/#p-963793).
